Guideline: Use useState for simple local state
Description: useState is ideal for primitive values and simple objects
Do: Use useState for form inputs and toggles
Don't: Use useState for complex nested state

Guideline: Use useReducer for complex state logic
Description: useReducer is better when state updates depend on previous state or involve multiple sub-values
Do: Use useReducer for form state with validation
Don't: Use useState with complex update logic

Description: Use function form to avoid expensive computations on every render
Do: Pass function to useState for expensive initial values
Don't: Compute expensive initial state inline

Description: Never mutate state directly - create new references
Do: Use spread operator or immer for updates
Don't: Mutate arrays or objects directly

Description: Missing dependencies cause stale closures and bugs
Do: Add all referenced values to dependency array
Don't: Omit dependencies to prevent re-runs

Guideline: Use cleanup functions to prevent memory leaks
Description: Return cleanup function from useEffect for subscriptions and timers
Do: Return cleanup for event listeners and intervals
Don't: Leave subscriptions open on unmount

Description: Compute derived values during render instead
Do: Calculate derived state inline or with useMemo
Don't: Store derived state and sync with useEffect

Description: useLayoutEffect runs synchronously after DOM mutations
Do: Use useLayoutEffect to measure element dimensions
Don't: Use useEffect for layout-dependent code

Guideline: Avoid creating objects in JSX props
Description: Object literals create new references on every render
Do: Define objects outside render or use useMemo
Don't: Pass object literals as props directly

Description: Wrap components that render often with same props
Do: Memoize components that receive stable props
Don't: Memoize every component by default

Guideline: Use key prop correctly in lists
Description: Keys help React identify which items changed
Do: Use stable unique IDs as keys
Don't: Use array index as key for dynamic lists

Guideline: Avoid inline function definitions in JSX
Description: Inline functions create new references causing child re-renders
Do: Use useCallback for event handlers passed to children
Don't: Define event handlers inline

Description: Each component should do one thing well
Do: Extract reusable logic into separate components
Don't: Create large components with many responsibilities

Description: Build complex UIs by combining simple components
Do: Pass children and render props for flexibility
Don't: Extend components through inheritance

Description: Keep state and effects close to where they are used
Do: Define hooks and handlers in the component that uses them
Don't: Lift all state to top-level components

Guideline: Use fragments to avoid wrapper divs
Description: Fragments let you group elements without extra DOM nodes
Do: Use <></> or <Fragment> for grouping
Don't: Add unnecessary wrapper divs

Description: Destructuring makes prop usage explicit
Do: Destructure props in function parameters
Don't: Access props with props.propName

Description: Set default props using ES6 default parameters
Do: Provide defaults in destructuring
Don't: Use defaultProps static property

Guideline: Avoid prop drilling with context or composition
Description: Pass data through component tree efficiently
Do: Use context for deeply shared state
Don't: Pass props through many intermediate components

Guideline: Spread remaining props with rest syntax
Description: Forward unknown props to underlying elements
Do: Use rest syntax for wrapper components
Don't: Manually forward each prop

Description: React wraps native events in SyntheticEvent
Do: Access event properties synchronously
Don't: Access event properties asynchronously

Description: Call preventDefault for form submissions and links
Do: Call e.preventDefault() when needed
Don't: Rely on return false to prevent default

Description: Use stopPropagation to prevent event bubbling
Do: Call e.stopPropagation() for nested handlers
Don't: Let events bubble unintentionally

Description: Handle events on parent elements for lists
Do: Add single handler on list container
Don't: Add handlers to every list item

Guideline: Use controlled components for form inputs
Description: Keep form state in React state
Do: Manage input value with useState
Don't: Use uncontrolled inputs with refs for all forms

Guideline: Use uncontrolled components for simple cases
Description: Refs are simpler for forms that only need values on submit
Do: Use refs for file inputs and simple forms
Don't: Use controlled inputs for one-time form submission
Code Bad: const [v, setV] = useState('') // for simple form

Guideline: Validate forms on blur and submit
Description: Show validation errors at appropriate times
Do: Validate onBlur and onSubmit
Don't: Validate only onChange causing excessive errors

Guideline: Use form libraries for complex forms
Description: Libraries like React Hook Form reduce boilerplate
Do: Use react-hook-form for complex multi-step forms
Don't: Build complex form logic from scratch

Description: Only call hooks at the top level and from React functions
Do: Call hooks unconditionally at component top level
Don't: Call hooks inside conditions loops or nested functions

Guideline: Create custom hooks for reusable logic
Description: Extract common patterns into custom hooks
Do: Create useLocalStorage useFetch useDebounce
Don't: Duplicate stateful logic across components

Guideline: Name custom hooks with use prefix
Description: The use prefix is required for hooks
Do: Name hooks useXxx to enable lint rules
Don't: Name hooks without use prefix

Description: useRef persists values without causing re-renders
Do: Use useRef for interval IDs and previous values
Don't: Use useState for values that do not affect render

Guideline: Use context for global app state
Description: Context is ideal for themes locale and user authentication
Do: Use context for theme user preferences auth state
Don't: Use context for frequently updating data

Description: Separate rarely-changing context from frequently-changing
Do: Create separate UserContext and UIContext
Don't: Put all state in one massive context

Description: Prevent unnecessary re-renders of context consumers
Do: Wrap context value in useMemo
Don't: Pass object literal as context value

Guideline: Create custom hooks for context consumption
Description: Provide a cleaner API for consuming context
Do: Export useAuth hook that uses useContext internally
Don't: Make consumers call useContext directly
Code Bad: const auth = useContext(AuthContext) // in every file

Description: Load components on demand to reduce bundle size
Do: Lazy load route-level components
Don't: Import all components eagerly

Description: Show fallback UI while lazy components load
Do: Wrap lazy components in Suspense with fallback
Don't: Show nothing while components load

Description: Measure actual performance before adding complexity
Do: Use React DevTools Profiler to find bottlenecks
Don't: Add useMemo useCallback everywhere preemptively
Code Good: // Profile showed ListItem is slow -> memo it

Description: Mark state updates as non-blocking for better UX
Do: Use useTransition for expensive re-renders
Don't: Block UI during heavy computations

Guideline: Use error boundaries for component errors
Description: Catch JavaScript errors in component tree
Do: Create ErrorBoundary class component
Don't: Let errors crash the whole app

Description: try-catch cannot catch errors in useEffect callbacks
Do: Handle errors inside async effect functions
Don't: Wrap useEffect in try-catch

Description: Display user-friendly error messages
Do: Show specific error message with recovery action
Don't: Show generic Error occurred message

Description: Write tests that resemble how users interact
Do: Use getByRole getByText for queries
Don't: Test internal state or implementation details

Description: RTL encourages accessible and maintainable tests
Do: Use render screen fireEvent from RTL
Don't: Use Enzyme or test internal component state

Description: Isolate components from network and side effects
Do: Mock fetch calls and external services
Don't: Make real API calls in unit tests

Description: Semantic elements provide built-in accessibility
Do: Use button for clickable actions
Don't: Use div with onClick for buttons

Guideline: Add ARIA labels for custom components
Description: Provide context for screen readers
Do: Add aria-label aria-labelledby for custom widgets
Don't: Omit accessibility attributes on interactive elements

Guideline: Manage focus for modals and dialogs
Description: Trap focus inside modals and return on close
Do: Use focus-trap library or manage focus manually
Don't: Let focus escape modal boundaries

Description: Use TypeScript interfaces for component props
Do: Define Props interface for each component
Don't: Use inline type annotations or any
Code Good: interface ButtonProps { label: string; onClick: () => void }

Description: Use correct event types for handlers
Do: Use React.MouseEvent React.ChangeEvent etc
Don't: Use any for event parameter

Guideline: Use render props for flexible components
Description: Share code between components using a render prop
Do: Use render prop for components needing render control
Don't: Duplicate rendering logic
Code Bad: // Same fetching logic in Display1 Display2

Guideline: Use compound components for related UI
Description: Build components that work together implicitly
Do: Create Select with Select.Option pattern
Don't: Pass all options as props array
